page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.large is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU architecture is arm64, configured with 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is 4.0 GB, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results show strong single-threaded performance in specific workloads, such as gzip compression at 30.3 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.33 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations are generally weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3400.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3237.0. Memory bandwidth is mixed, registering a weak 23.11 GB/sec for large-block writes but an average 55.79 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations on Passmark score in the average tier at 3036.36, while Redis SET operations achieve 4.53 million operations per second.

Given its dedicated ARMv9 cores and low memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and lightweight database operations that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is suitable for utility computing, text processing, and lightweight containerized microservices, though it is less optimal for multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.large is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It is equipped with 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) operating at 2.8 GHz, 4.0 GB of system memory, and a 4 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, particularly in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside average performance in text processing and database operations. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, memory write bandwidth, and software compilation tasks fall into weaker performance tiers. This resource profile makes the instance a qualitatively cost-effective option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and simple database operations, while remaining less suited for memory-intensive or highly parallelized computing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.large is a compute-optimized arm64 instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, 4 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong single-threaded performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.large is a compute-optimized arm64 instance with 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARMv9-based compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores for efficient single-threaded processing and lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores
4.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression tasks
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and microservices
